children.heading: Submission of project proposals to Secretary concerned
children.label: a Submission of project proposals to Secretary concerned

children.heading: Projects funded using project funds
children.label: 1 Projects funded using project funds
children.text: Not later than September 30 of each fiscal year (or a later date specified by the Secretary concerned for the fiscal year), each resource advisory committee shall submit to the Secretary concerned a description of any projects that the resource advisory committee proposes the Secretary undertake using any project funds reserved by eligible counties in the area in which the resource advisory committee has geographic jurisdiction.

children.heading: Projects funded using other funds
children.label: 2 Projects funded using other funds
children.text: A resource advisory committee may submit to the Secretary concerned a description of any projects that the committee proposes the Secretary undertake using funds from State or local governments, or from the private sector, other than project funds and funds appropriated and otherwise available to do similar work.

children.heading: Joint projects
children.label: 3 Joint projects
children.text: Participating counties or other persons may propose to pool project funds or other funds, described in paragraph (2), and jointly propose a project or group of projects to a resource advisory committee established under .section 7125 of this title
The purpose of the project and a description of how the project will meet the purposes of this subchapter.
The anticipated duration of the project.
The anticipated cost of the project.
The proposed source of funding for the project, whether project funds or other funds.

children.label: A
children.text: Expected outcomes, including how the project will meet or exceed desired ecological conditions, maintenance objectives, or stewardship objectives.

children.label: B
children.text: An estimate of the amount of any timber, forage, and other commodities and other economic activity, including jobs generated, if any, anticipated as part of the project.

children.label: A
children.text: tracks and identifies the positive or negative impacts of the project, implementation, and provides for validation monitoring; and1So in original.1

children.label: i
children.text: Whether or not the project met or exceeded desired ecological conditions; created local employment or training opportunities, including summer youth jobs programs such as the Youth Conservation Corps where appropriate.

children.label: ii
children.text: Whether the project improved the use of, or added value to, any products removed from land consistent with the purposes of this subchapter.
An assessment that the project is to be in the public interest.

children.heading: Authorized projects
children.label: c Authorized projects
children.text: Projects proposed under subsection (a) shall be consistent with .section 7101 of this title
